research_question: Can joint reward+hand contrastive supervision learn a multi-axis representation that encodes both strategic value and opponent identity?
key_findings: Dual-axis construction prevents 1D collapse: 2 effective dims vs 1 in hand_identity_repr_v1
Both signals encoded: 53.8% opp hand accuracy (above 50% target), significant reward Spearman rho
Reward Spearman rho (0.107) below reward-only baseline (0.163) — capacity shared between axes
InfoNCE loss diverged to -inf after VICReg variance term went to 0 (~7000 episodes)
Loss instability did not prevent meaningful representation learning — diagnostics confirm real structure
follow_up: dual_axis_repr_v2: address InfoNCE instability via increased lambda_var, embedding norm clipping, or stop-gradient
